feed.title: Iris' Wonderland
feed.subtitle: 雨天的艾莉丝和奇幻仙境

entry.title: A Chinese Style Horror Game
entry.content:
This game is the last one I made in the MA Game Design course.

Compared to the first one, there are indeed many improvements.

Thank David for all his valuable advice.

Why I made this game

At the very beginning, I just wanted a horror game with all the techniques I learned in this course. I planed to use some Chinese elements, but not as the protagonist. Then one thing happened in the reality change my mind —— the Hong Kong protest.

Actually, before that event, I didn’t have a clear attitude in political topics about China.

Although in the game about the Cultural revolution, I blamed the Chinese government for the mistakes and tragedy it once brought to thousands of people. And in daily chat, I often complained about its strict censorship in films and games.

However, when I ask myself whether this government did a terrible job since I was born, I should say with no exaggeration that it has improved a lot compared to the 1960s and is now the best of the world in many aspects.

In my grandma’s generation, people suffered a lot from starvation and poverty. In my mother’s generation, things got better. However, people left far away from home not to realize their dreams but just to make a living. Now, I can study abroad and learn how to make games. That’s the difference.

Each time when I go back home, I feel that I am outdated because of the new constructions and new technology applied to daily life. Of course, London is beautiful and attractive. However, comparing to my small city developing at such a high speed, I can’t tell which one is better now not to say in the next ten years.

Back to the Hong Kong protest, I can understand HKers’ fear of losing ‘freedom’ and other reasons against mainland China. However, I can not tolerate my homeland and a government that is actually doing well being slandered with rumors and fake news.

My game is made to promote Chinese culture and convey my thoughts about ideological conflict. The story is about ordinary people and ‘superpowers’. These two groups of people fight and demonize each other. When the power comparison changes, the attitude switches. It is similar to the west compared to China, or Hong Kong compared to the mainland.

The title and the endings

Prisoner

‘Prisoner’ or prison in Chinese is the character ‘囚’, which is a pictogram of a man stands in a square. The ‘square’ could be interpreted as four walls around in vertical or a corridor in horizontal like in my game.

‘Prisoner’ refers to the player who was kidnapped and sent to the facility. It also represents the core of the facility —— another psychokinesis which is much similar to the player but has a different belief.

Two endings

If the player doesn’t collect all the files, the ending is that the player touches the egg and becomes the new core of the facility because of the lack of information about the fight.

Another ending is that the player reaches the egg and conveys all the info to the core and spreads that to other facilities. And all psychokinesis decide to fly to the moon to find a new home and avoid the endless fight with ordinary people.

The moon

The moon in the game also has two meanings.

One is similar to W. Somerset Maugham’s The Moon and Sixpence. The moon is high hanging there, far from touching. It is the dream of absolute freedom and many other beautiful but not real ideas. The main character Strickland does achieve his goal in the end but sacrifice many people behind. Many HKers are like Strickland. They do fight for something lofty. However, they don’t know and don’t care there are still millions of people fighting for their sixpence to live.

Another meaning is the real moon in the good ending. The psychokinesis flies to the moon to build a new homeland. That is also my answer to solving the problems nowadays in the world between the superpower countries. All the problems on earth are about resources. The western countries see the competition as a zero-sum game. The Chinese government wants to convince them to believe at least nowadays it could be a win-win game. However, in the long term, it is still a zero-sum game. So, the only solution for our human-beings is developing the technology to explore outer space. And China is now putting great effort in this area.

Prisoners are we human-beings trapped on the earth and fight against ourselves.

Elements in the game

The Fengshui Compass

The Fengshui Compass is a fortune-telling prop like a crystal ball. It can be used to divine how to settle a house and how to place pieces of furniture to have good luck. With a Magnet spoon, it is able to function as a real Compass. So, in the game, it is for illumination and reveals secrets.

The Chinese sedan chair and color

The sedan chair was a traditional vehicle in ancient China. It was often used by officers or wealthy landowners. But its function is not limited to transport: The sedan is linked with one of the important ceremonies in life —— marriage.

Different from the west, red is the main color for weddings in China. Instead, white is for the funeral. What’s interesting is that both white and red are often seen in horror movies in both China and the west.

And in ancient China, a custom brings those two situations together —— ghost wedding. It was often forced or semi-forced for no one willing to do so.

The Granny Meng’s soup and Naihe Bridge

Chinese culture is a mix of Confucianism, Buddhism, and Taoism. The reincarnation in Buddhism has a great influence on China and surrounding countries for thousands of years.

In Chinese folklore, before people reborn into another life, they need to drink the Granny Meng’s soup to forget everything and across the Naihe bridge.

The Egg

The egg symbolizes the new life and another reincarnation. No completely broken shell symbolizes fragile but stubborn belief.

About technical problems and findings

  1. Unity supports adding Alembic assets to the project and importing Alembic (.abc) files. However, when using abc file in the project, there can’t be fbx objects as its child. It may cause models missing.
  2. Changing platforms or using more than one camera may cause post-processing not working in build. The solution on the Internet is to delete the camera and recreate one. But it is time-consuming when there are scripts attached or related to the camera or there are too many scenes. In fact, you can solve the problem by making Unity reassemble the project by switching the architecture or switching the platforms twice.
entry.summary:
This game is the last one I made in the MA Game Design course.

Compared to the first one, there are indeed many improvements.

Thank David for all his valuable advice.

entry.title: A Story about My Grandma
A Fake AR Project
entry.content:
Introduction

The game is a (fake) AR project that allows players to discover a magical world in the mobile and see the real world at the same time.

This project targets at these three points.

  1. test a new flow to make 3D games with MagicaVoxel.

  2. test how much the real world elements affect players.

  3. analysis if jump scare still works if using a cute art style.

Gameplay

Players can wipe away mist in the scene to promote the development of the plot. A small story would slower appear. At the end of the game, a monster would jump into the scene without any precursor.

Story

It is like a small comic. The prototype of the story comes from the lunar tower and solar tower in dark souls 2.

Case study and Fake AR

Case study

There are many “AR” sky apps like Sky map and Star Walk 2. These apps are designed to teach basic astronomy knowledge – or a good trick for a date. Because it is incredible to look at the sky, seeing stars shining clearly in modern cities. It is like you jump in the rabbit hole, falling on the halfway to the magic world. Many AR games typically lack a close crossmedia integration of the surroundings, and often annotate or extend the environment rather than modifying and altering it(Herbst et al., 2019). However, in this case, it combines with the environment well. It is stimulating and enjoyable. This experience interests me to discover how the real world elements affect players.

Fake AR

One of the similarities of these apps is that they all have a function called augmented reality mode. However, the truth is that they don’t use the AR kit to match the pictures or models with the sky. All they need is to detect the angle of the phone using the gyroscope. This method spares me much time to concentrate on other parts of the game. ( actually, there are some complex parts about the angle and the stable movement of the camera.)

New Workflow

In this project, I didn’t use the modeling and material painter tools before. I try to use MagicaVoxel to build the scene. The advancement of this tool is light – it is exceptionally suitable to do light or mobile project for the following reasons.

  1. The modeling in this software is to add blocks like the toy blocks. It is easy to understand and easy to learn.

  2. Because models are just blocks. The size of the file is tiny.

  3. The paint procedure is simple and can export materials.

  4. After some practice, the outcome can be good looking.

Art style

As I mentioned in the beginning, all the models in the game should be cute. So, to make the game like a dark fairy tale, I tried to adjust the lights and shadows to form a sharp contrast.

And, this game is different from the other projects before. It runs on mobile, and my phone is inferior. So, it is kind of hard to reach a balance between the performance and the quality of images.

Reflection

The thinking and result of test 2

User feels that the game presentation with 3D and AR technology is more interesting than general 2D game, the immersion of virtual object in real world also brings unique game ambient and touch-screen gameplay makes the player becomes more engaged to the game(Santoso, 2019). This makes a paradox that when a player more engaged into a game, how they feel the real world in AR case.

In the play test, 5 in 10 people saw that I made a face to them when the transparency of the camera is 60%. However, when the transparency is 0, everyone noticed my actions.

It’s like the real world has become a part of the game experience.

Problems

  1. Many players response that it is tired and hard to raise the phone to see the sky. It may because my participants are almost all programmers or designer who sit all day long. Comparing to the AR sky apps, they suggest users to lying on the ground to explore the sky, while my app requires players to turn around. So, it may be not proper to set high targets in my case.
  1. Some players can’t find the right route to finish the game. Although I have made at least one guide in each part, like the ship encountering, the queen falling and the musical note flying, it is still a bit hard to find the target because of the following reasons.

a. all the elements are in one scene. So players can be distracted by the environment like the tree.

b. the targets are scattered around the map, and the angle is three dimensions.

c. the mist to cover the models cannot be load properly until the camera targets directly at the mist – I still don’t know how to fix it.

I tried serval ways to give more guidance to players, like David’s advice to add a bird or butterfly to lead the camera. However, it seems that the bird can only be close to the camera to be seen and functions just like an arrow to indicate the enemy in an action game. The logic is not flying to the target but to point the position in the screen space. So, I am stilling trying to program that properly.

  1. The monster at the end of the game is not scary enough. Actually, there is about 40 percent of participants being sacred. And, I noticed that it depends on whether the volume is high enough. In this game, to test if the jump scare works well, the environment is totally normal as the control group. So, I hope in the final project, when the environment becomes weird, the whole game would become scarier.

(I like the monster after all.)

entry.title: Raindrops keep falling on my head (Unity Archive Game)
entry.content:
Finally, it’s time to make a video game! However, it seems that the time is limited and the holiday is approaching… So, I just finished the game but left nothing recorded. The content below will be almost the same as the report, except a video of an unfinished version and the detailed information on level design, which I forget to discuss. Hmmm, forgive me!

Introduction

Generally, it is a 3D parkour game with a small but obscure story. All interactions are using WASD to control a robot to evade objects flying by. There is a force pushing the robot down, and the robot will automatically stop without getting gears, so keep moving until reach the end. There is the truth waiting for you.

Motivation

I want to make a relatively complete game. So, in such a short time, it is suitable to choose simple mechanisms. I also want to present something of myself – I always use iris as the nickname online because of a light novel. Therefore, I may retell the story with my game.

Case-Study

Iris in the rain

‘Iris in the rain’ is one of my favorite light novels. It tells a story about a robot girl called ‘Iris’ accidentally abandoned by her master. Her ‘heart’ was put into a cleaning robot like Wall-E. Because the monitor was broken, she could see black lines moving through her view like it was raining day and night.

Actually, I don’t know why I am so into the novel. I just dreamt about the plot and thought it is part of mine since I am in middle school. The broken gears of the robot or the endless rain give me the feeling of sorrow and beauty. I always want to display the scenes with my works.​

Fairyland Story

It is a 2D parkour game made by a couple telling a fairytale of the little girl. The color and the shape of the elements are lovely. Also, the mechanisms are just getting stars and not hitting on edge.

The background of the game is turning around, and the character move vertical, which seems like the character is on a stage. It adds the feeling of a fairytale but a bit childish for what I want.

In the beginning, I might make a 2D platform jumping game. However, I cannot find a suitable object in the archive. After coming across this, I want to use a similar mechanism but make it more dangerous and exciting.

Steampunk Asset

It is an asset I find in the unity store. I want to learn and improve the skill of how to build and render the scene. Also, I know in my game, there are gears and rain. To make the elements contrast with each other and form the feeling of both soft and hard, the art style of steampunk is suitable. So, I pick this asset as a reference. However, the real quality of it is not satisfied and what I got, at last, may just the steam to add atmosphere.

Making process

Modeling and animation

I chose some of the objects in the archive to build my tunnel and the obstructions. I copy, twist and combine them together to form a large machine. The seem-to-be endless tunnel gives players pressure and changing these peaceful objects into terrible shape.

The animation of the character is a bit stiff because its body is made of gears and cannot be animated in a regular way. Moreover, I fail to make the animation of the character spinning when she gets gears. (It would cause some problem with the camera.)

The gears also have been animated. However, the camera moves too fast to catch that…

Programming

Because the mechanisms are simple, so I haven’t spent much time on thinking how to write. However, the idea of want to make a complete game is time-consuming. Everything from the beginning to the end should be considered.

(The script to let the character move around the center of the screen may be my brand-new invention. Aha!)

Design

color

The grey and heavy background are formed with the objects twisted or combined in the archive. The obstruction and the character are bright and shining to contrast with the background.

I used to choose orange, blue and green as the primary color. However, in order to clearly present that the character gets the gears, I change the character into purple.

light

There are three directional light and many point light to make the shadows complicated enough to add realism. On the contrary, the blooming effects and bright object make the scene unreal.

character

The robot girl was a small one built with gears. After playtests, many players don’t know whether they are controlling the girl or the raindrop and even don’t she is a girl. Plus, it’s hard to see the animation of the character. So, then I change her into an order one.​

levels

The main obstruction change shapes and the spin speed. Also, the distribution of gears is different and on the half way the force of rain will increase. The difficulty was different with these four factors changing. The gradient of difficulty is like this:

The third area is full of malice. The first half force players to move throw the holes of blade otherwise iris will lose speed; The second half cannot slow down to carefully move across fast blades due to too many gears.

Optimization

The first version of the game is too big because of the model - I made the long tunnel manually. To solve that, I cut it down into pieces, turn certain degrees and connect them together. After that, the game is about 1/2 of the original.

Playtest

Playtest 1

The first playtest was done when the game was almost finished. However, I received two distinct evaluations. Edward thought it is too hard to continue and disappointed with the guidance. Mika could not move long distance as well, but he likes the art style and thought it’s a lovely game.

So, I tried to introduce the unclear part with words or animation. Also, I enlarge the detect area of gears to make it easier to get.

Playtest 2

The main complaints are still about difficulty. Then players also think the music does not much the picture.

However, I don’t want to change either of them.

About the difficulty, I don’t think it’s that frustrated. If it is entirely due to inadequate control, I may change my mind.

About the music, I want it to increase the feeling of contrast, and it matches the rhythm of push W.

Playtest 3

It helps to find some bugs:

  1. Characters can move after death.

  2. The animation does not work under certain conditions.

  3. Animation plays faster than expected.

Reflection

This project teaches me serval truth below:

  1. It’s hard to make a complete game and make it in an elegant way. My code is terrible, and the way to make tunnel is also kind of stupid.

  2. I should record things in time because sometimes ideas and thoughts just slipped away.

  3. Never write the report right before the deadline.
entry.summary: Finally, it’s time to make a video game! However, it seems that the time is limited and the holiday is approaching… So, I just finished the game but left nothing recorded. The content below will be almost the same as the report, except a video of an unfinished version and the detailed information on level design, which I forget to discuss. Hmmm, forgive me!

entry.title: Wuxia & Games (Understanding Gaming Experience)
entry.content:
It’s a relatively hard decision to choose Wuxia video games as the subject of the essay. Because there are few studies about this topic in Western academia and even the same in Chinese academia. However, it is an exciting adventure for me to discover such an area that I can not avoid and both love and hate with powerful emotion.

Motivation

I used to kind of hate the Wuxia video games. Because there are too many in China, almost 7 in 10 games are with the label of Wuxia. Moreover, less than 1 in 10 is relatively good.

However, last year two events changed my mind. One is that a Wuxia novel master Jingyong died in 10.30. I love his books and feel great sorrow for his death. Another is that finally, a Wuxia game attracts me so much. It is called The Scroll of Taiwu. I admit that It is not a masterpiece but contains many innovations like the level up mechanism is diverse and unique; each NPC has their own stories recorded; each setting with Chinese culture is carefully examined … Its disadvantages are also very obvious – terrible newbie guide, inappropriate interface, and interactions, and repeated level-up progress. However, flaws cannot obscure the splendor of the jade.

Why Wuxia is charming

Wuxia, known as “martial heroes” in Chinese culture, has significant influences on different media of art in East Asia, including literature, painting, film, TV series and game.

For me, Wuxia is attractive mainly for its culture, which contains history for thousands of years. The world it draws, the heroes it describes and the sense of feeling it transfers all hold the thick passion of Chinese romance. Plus, in almost all kinds of media, Wuxia can always show unique characteristics, which attract many fans interested in different areas.

Take skills in Wuxia games as an example. In Western RPG or JRPG, skills may have names like “fireball” or “fire bolt” or (cooler) “ultimate flame burst.” These names are direct and easy to remember, but lack of connotations. In Wuxia game, a recovery skill may be called (direct translate)”Nine-color Jade cicada spell.” In Chinese culture, cicada flying away from its shell represents the rebirth and jade can obtain people’s souls. “Nine-color” functions similarly as “ultimate.” Although it causes problems when players are not in the cultural context. However, for Chinese, it is fascinating to play such games that only a skill could be this meaningful.

Wuxia Games

The writer is too lazy to put more words here.

Samurai & Wuxia

These are some pictures taken from CG animation in Wuxia or Samurai games. I conducted research with 23 Wuxia novel lovers and 27 online Wuxia game players. These people are familiar with Wuxia story and very confident that they can tell the difference between Wuxia and Samurai.

However, the result is that only 3 of them can judge it perfectly right. Moreover, the three admitted that it was possible because of luck. Although, I also have to admit it is hard to recognize them according to the scenery. Still, it could reflect the similarity (at least in art style) in these two kinds of games.
entry.summary: It’s a relatively hard decision to choose Wuxia video games as the subject of the essay. Because there are few studies about this topic in Western academia and even the same in Chinese academia. However, it is an exciting adventure for me to discover such an area that I can not avoid and both love and hate with powerful emotion.

entry.title: 对话
entry.content:
这里白茫茫的一片，以我为中心，由近及远都是纯粹的白色。

我环顾四周，这些白色竟不尽相同。

前方的白色湿漉漉的。可能原来是一弯湖水，但水渐渐退去，剩下干瘪的河床。奄奄一息的鱼虾吐出无数泡沫，从水底一直垒到天上，便成了白色。

左边有芳草的味道。可能从前是一片草场，但牛羊越聚越多，站都站不下，堆了四五层，轮流伏在地上吃草。草吃光了，牛羊都死了，成了白骨。

右边凉飕飕的，还有风。可能是一座山，现在就立在那里，很高很高。我在山尖，所以满是云和雪。

我转过身去，霎时孤独极了，因为我什么都感受不到，那里真的什么都没有。

Ⅰ

正当我沉浸在悲伤里，虚无中猛地勾勒出一张脸，准确的说是一个带黑色描边和灰色阴影的白圆（看似立体，实则平面），像是渲染器里的卡通材质球。

果然，它说话了，“你好。”

“你……好。”我没多考虑，只是想回话。

“你看起来很有趣。”它的声音抑扬顿挫，很好听，似乎比我更有感情。

“是吗……但我很伤心。”

“你看起很聪明。”

“可我总犯蠢。”

“你会忘记的，会像我一样快乐。”

我有些感动，也有些困惑，“谢……谢你。”

……

我们一同前行，到了一处“深渊”前——愈向下愈深的黑暗横梗在几步远处，向视线两端无限延展开去，仅有一条细细的白线通向另一侧。

“你是个勇敢的人，我看好你。”她语气坚定。

我颤巍巍地答道，“但……太高了我有些害怕。”

她头也不回径直飘走了，“你快跟过来，我等你。”

“好，我再努力一下。”

“我先去劈柴，你过来之后帮我烧水。”

什么是“柴”？什么是“水”？可我无暇顾及这些，只有死死揪住白线朝前挪动。

我喘着粗气，到了对岸，“呼，呼……我喜欢你。”

“这边杂音太大。”

“我喜欢你！”卯足了劲，又是一嗓子。

“我知道，在烧水了吗？”她语气一如往常。

“我有点伤心。”

“你哪里都好啊。”

“不好！”

“你不伤心的时候总是很有趣。”

语毕，我又掉过头向着湿漉漉的地方前进，“深渊”竟也消失不见了。

Ⅱ

不知走了多久，身后又传来了窸窸窣窣的响声。我想了想，无奈地转过头去，空无一物。

于是又继续上路，可没走多久那声音又紧跟了过来。再回头，只瞥见一个黑影，闪出了视线外。我转了下脑袋，又看见了那团黑影，随即它跳到了视野另一边，我的视线也跟了过去。这样反复了几个回合，它终于败下阵来，仿佛有些沮丧地靠近我。

这回是个有白高光的黑色“材质球”（同样平面）。

“我们认识？”我一脸疑惑。

“认识！”它声音有些沙哑。

“什么时候？”

“刚刚啊，你陪我玩了。”

“啊？哦。”是指把它逼出来这件事？

“我能跟着你吗？”

我并不在意，“随你吧。”

“太好了。”它一副很开心的样子。

……

没过多久，我们不得不停住了。那“深渊”竟又出现在了眼前。

我盘腿坐在地上，沉默了一会儿，哇地哭了出来。

“你很难过吧。”黑“球”靠了过来，边沿抵在我肩膀上，有些疼。

“我以前干了很多蠢事。”

“我知道。”她嗓音干瘪却有力。

“我不勇敢，我害怕。”哭得更厉害了。

“是啊，你多胆小啊。”她转了下身子，平着贴过来。

“我既狡猾又卑劣。”

“我知道。”

“我虚伪又爱嫉妒。”

“可不是嘛。”她温柔地蹭了蹭我的头发，又很艰难地把自己弯曲起来，像是在抱我。

我有一会儿没说出话，然后挤出一句，“我害怕被抛弃，所以总是自己离开。”

“我会一直跟着。”她对答如流。

“我目光短浅。”

“我知道。”

“我固执片面。”

“我知道。”

……

“我讨厌自己。”

“可我喜欢你啊，全部的你，一切好的坏的。”

我的脸颊被啄了一下，原来肩上停了一只金丝雀。眼前的深渊先是化作了泡沫，泡沫又沉淀下来成了大海，海的尽头是天空。我久久驻足，同金丝雀一起歌唱，“潮涨潮落，日升月落……”

我环顾四周，这些白色竟不尽相同。

左边有芳草的味道。可能从前是一片草场，但牛羊越聚越多，站都站不下，堆了四五层，轮流伏在地上吃草。草吃光了，牛羊都死了，成了白骨。

右边凉飕飕的，还有风。可能是一座山，现在就立在那里，很高很高。我在山尖，所以满是云和雪。

我转过身去，霎时孤独极了，因为我什么都感受不到，那里真的什么都没有。

entry.title: 雾都的雨天没有下雪
entry.content:
“风……

在吹……

雨……

在下……

人……

在走……”

古龙先生这段话甚是好用。

来伦敦已将近半年，传说中仅仅五天的晴天也已经历了五天又五个多月。这里比起家乡并不多雨，以至于想起雨来反倒满脑子都是初高中梅雨时节那些数不清上不成的体育课。

今天下起了一场雨。普通得很，普通到任何想要描述它的句子好像都会冷不丁地出现在随手翻开的一本书里。于是，我取出了桌边的kindle，抿了抿手指，装出翻开书页的样子按下了开机键。《刺杀骑士团长》的阅读进度停在了49%。这书里大概会有吧。我不确定。

其实开篇第一章第三行就有雨，可书是英文版，写的是“Rain”。我不满足，不服气，毕竟那不完全是“雨”啊。所以我希望在后半本的英文里找出“雨”来。

不管哪里的雨天都适合读书。阴沉的天，看不见太阳，不有意看表就把不准时间，等外面天完全暗了，才想起把头从书堆里拽出来。要是在夏天，太阳落得晚，内容不太晦涩的小书只消一日就读完了。可手头这本不愧是说英语的“骑士团长”，我才勉强把进度向前推了十个点，脑袋就有些涨，肚子也抗议起来，只能出门觅食。

目的地是离家两朵云远的中餐馆。雨下着，路人行色匆匆——只有零星几个打着伞，其余的要么用帽兜罩着，要么同我一样在雨里跑着。“光明正大”地“不打伞”大概是我来这里最快乐的事。不要误解我受到了什么虐待，（除了吃的，）一切都很好，只是淋雨对我意义非凡。一是，我不喜欢伞，“伞”的造型延伸下去像是带刺的壳，无论伞里塞了几个人，终究是排外的；二来，雨是一种馈赠，于我于万物生灵都是，我愿意敞开心扉——至少敞着脑袋接受；三是，雨水从脸颊划过让我觉得自己真实地活着——我知道雨帘里我占用的那部分空间就是自己的外形，雨水坠在身上的声响就是我的话语，雨滴的颜色就是我的颜色……

回去的路上，雨依旧下着。淋湿了的圣诞促销广告提醒我已近年末。今年圣诞会下雪吗？我还是满心期待——算是对眼前的雨的小小的背叛吧，可在没下雪的此刻，我的全身心都是属于这场雨的。

来自12月31日的剧透：

今年，最终没有下雪。

如果用天气总结一下这半年，大概是：“许多晴天，偶尔有雾，时不时下雨。虽然最后都没有下雪，但有雨就很好，希望这次这雨不要停歇。“
entry.summary:
“风……

在吹……

雨……

在下……

人……

在走……”

古龙先生这段话甚是好用。

entry.title: Brave New Room (The Escape Room Project)
entry.content:
O wonder!
How many goodly creatures are there here!
How beauteous mankind is! O brave new world,
That has such people in’t.

— William Shakespeare, The Tempest, Act V, Scene I, ll. 203–206

Before 11.1

At the very beginning, after finishing exploring our room, I think of using the room itself to make puzzles. For example, one side of the wall was different from the other three. There are concaves suitable for hiding the long and thin item. Because we have decided to set the background as a dark room, I think maybe red light sticks can easily be fixed in the concaves and hardly be found when the red lights on.

And as in this case, lights off are needed. So, maybe UV light and invisible ink can be used to indicate turning off the light. The pictures to cover the message beneath can also be used as the carrier of the invisible ink.

Another idea is about the mat already placed on the ground. Because it looks like the chess board, I think maybe we can give clues about the endgame and let players calculate how to checkmate. If the endgame is complicated enough, we can even provide some items, so that players can act as pieces and cooperate.

11.2

Multiple exposure was very popular among young Chinese in 2015. I think this tech can be used to connect different information together.

11.3

Today we share our puzzles with teammates for the first time. I find that I may not be good at making puzzles, but I can give probably good advice (ง •̀_•́)ง┻━┻

Just skip the advice part.

The two puzzles I made before meet different problems.

The UV light one seems to be too simple and does not match the theme very well.

As the chess one, I have to assume that everyone in the world knows the rules of chess or find another chess-like game which is worldwide popular. And even if the endgame is complicated, someone can also figure out how to solve in his or her mind.

By the way, one of my teammates’ puzzle can be solved by trial and error. I think it can be improved in some way. This reminds me of P and NP problems, Prime factor decomposition and then RSA algorithm. However, at last, we can not find a proper solution. Maybe she will talk about it in her report.

11.4

We talk about how to make cooperation puzzles today. Mika raises the idea of someone holding a button to control a light at the other end of the room and the light revealing something. The idea is simple and there are many similar variants which can be classified as physically impossible tasks.

(It reminds of a project I have done before. http://rainyiris.cc/2018/02/25/Portfolio/#more The last one.

It is designed for single, but sometimes people just cheat to cooperate.)

They encourage cooperation because we are not Doctor Octopus or Straw Hat Luffy.

My previous idea of chess may be considered as trying to make a mentally impossible task but a bit hard to practice. So, are there any other conditions that even the puzzle is within everyone’s reach but cannot be solved alone?

I look back to the room to find inspirations. The only furniture in our escape room is a small table with a headphone on it. So, I think of that maybe I could play different audios to give different information at the same time and let players use the information to solve the puzzle.

So, I make this small matching game. Just listen to the name of different capitals and fill them in the blanks in order. There are eight cities and each time the order and sound in each ear are different, so it is hard four one person to accomplish. However, when two players listen to their own side, they just need to remember four names and do the matching work.

As an industrial designer, not a programmer before, I seldom write code. So, in order to make the audios random properly, I do some research and find some interesting thing.

Companies like Spotify use different solutions to random their songs. And to ensure their customs feel that their lists are truly random, the systems are designed to change the order of some songs with same singer or writer after the random process.

It means that to make people think it is true, we make it fake ( ´◔ ‸◔’)

I don’t know if there is a similar system in game. For example, a hero in MOBA whose crit rate is 30%, and after it attacks 10 times without crit, the next time the crit rate will still be 30%. People will think it is not truly random, because it seems crit never occurs sometimes and they would remember these situations very clearly.

So, maybe the process is that let the crit rate be 20% and after each attack the rate rises with the whole rate close to 30%.

11.5

We talk about the story of the room today. Mika wants it to be a dystopia one.

And because we have set it a darkroom, the owner of the room should be a photographer.

So the story may go like this.

The old photographer was born in the old world before the dystopia. He traveled around the world and took many beautiful pictures. Then He attended the war resulting in the dystopia and became a hero. Before he died, he gave information to the rebel that there is key to overthrow the government in his darkroom. All the tasks in the room is a test to see if there is hope for this decaying society. And the key may be one of his valued photos (or all his collection).

(Add a time bar and shuffling animation to the coop puzzle.)

Recently I find a truth that the best poems cannot be transferred into novels and the best dramas cannot be transferred into films, because they make all use of their advantages in their media. What about games? I think we still have not reached the ceiling of games and there are many possibilities. But, just imagine an ideal game and what should it compare to? Life maybe.

11.6

Because I didn’t have a suitable earphone for my puzzle, I didn’t test it yesterday. In the test today, I find that the audios do not work. I dealt with the audios in Adobe AU. I just made them two-channel stereo and deleted one of it. However, the sound in the other channel can still be heard. It is really weird.

I ask my friend who majors in sound art for help. It is funny that she finds the same problem in her previous works as well. We try a lot of solutions, but none of it works.

Luckily, another friend who uses a mac book can just hear one sound and indicates that the problem is about the system but not the audio.

Finally, we find that reverberation in Windows 10 is off in default setting (ง •̀_•́)ง┻━┻

11.7

In today’s test, some players can’t find cities like Athens, Cairo and Tokyo.

Some of them confuse Athens with Rome, and the most interesting thing is none of them are Chinese.

In Chinese history lessons, students are supposed to tell different architecture styles in ancient Greece and Rome like Doric Order, Ionic Order and Corinthian Order.

As to Cairo, it seems that many people are familiar with pyramid and Egypt. However, they don’t know Cairo is the capital of Egypt.

When it comes to Tokyo, I think that I may have chosen an inappropriate picture.

I considered to use Tokyo Tower or Sky Tree as the symbol of Tokyo, but I was afraid that players would confuse them with La Tour Eiffel or Oriental Pearl TV Tower. I don’t want to use cherry blossom or shinto shrine to represent Tokyo, because they are part of Japanese culture but not primarily for Tokyo. For me, they are more like Kyoto things. Then, I ask my teammates and classmates about whether they can figure out Tokyo Tower. Most of them think it is quite easy. So, I change the left photo to the right one.

11.8

I have not given up the first two ideas. Especially the UV light one can be very suitable for the darkroom. I simplify the idea of hiding the UV light behind a photo and making a big reverse to just putting the UV light on an exposed surface and hiding some tips or functioning as an import for another puzzle.

Also, thank Edward’s idea of this classic setting↑

11.9

Because our room will be in a dark red background, we consider how to generate red light and how to make puzzles readable.

It is very interesting that my Chinese teammates think of using translucent film to cover the lights almost at the same time, but Richard and other European students think that it would cause the lights heat. I think there may be some difference in the education system or culture which produces Chinese tending to be practical but not careful. As an industrial designer and have tried this solution before, I know it would work properly.

As to the puzzles, there are quite a lot of puzzles in our darkroom requiring players to read. However, the illumination condition is terrible. So, we try to simplify them much easier as they can be. I also help Monica make her puzzle a tablet version. The reason why we don’t just print them out is for the consideration of readability.

I choose Android system and generate a small program to carry those pictures so that there is no physical Home button and cannot log out except for shutting down.

11.10-11.8

In the following days, we decorate the room and conduct many tests and I can’t remember what happened on which day.

Decoration

This part works very well. It is the first time that I am just benefited from my height. I thought my experience in decoration company would help, but it seems that Monica is more considerate. So, I just need to follow her instruction and stick everything correctly.

The UV Light and Mika’s puzzle

Unfortunately, on the first day of the test, no one solved the UV light and Mika’s puzzle. No matter how obvious the light put or how direct the hint set, there seemed to be too many distracters. So, we deleted them the next day.

I reconsidered a lot about why no one can find the truth behind the timer. My classmates told me if adding a block under the timer, players would know the computer are able to interact with. However, I have thought about it before and why I didn’t do that is I want players to consider it as a timer at the beginning and then find that it is more than a timer later. If I use another hint to indicate that, it would be better just to delete the timer.

As for Mika’s puzzle, we take David’s advice to replace the UV light as the import of the audio puzzle. We learn lessons from the first day’s fault and make the interface as clear as it can be. I also let the input numbers shine to indicate that it can interact and borrow the keypad from another group to narrow down the interactable area.

In the last day’s test, all three groups solve the puzzle and even give high ratings to it.

About story

Because the ending has changed to reveal a piece of paper, the dystopia story was is no longer in use. Richard’s detective setting attracts many interests and brings much fun to our escape room.

I think the story now is not entirely suitable for all the puzzles and some parts are even not reasonable, but it does achieve great success.

This makes me rethink the relationship between a game and its narrative.

I will not burden you with a lengthy account.

The audio puzzle

The original intention of making this puzzle is straightforward. I did not expect players would consider it as a good one. Since no one played the puzzle on the first day, I even thought it might not work.

However, it is fortunate that the last two days, three out of six groups think it’s the best puzzle in the room and no one complain that it is terribly designed. They give different kinds of reasons why it is good and some of them I have never thought about it before. It’s amazing that players can find a lot more things than the designer.

There are many changes to the puzzle like the picture, the audio, the hints in the background, the time limit, the redo icon, and the shuffling animation. All these changes contain so much consideration and many decisions.

For example, I delete the shuffling animation many times and re-add it many times. The shuffle ends when the audio stop, but before that, some locations have been fixed. This may cause confusions to players. However, I don’t want players to react when they are still listening. This would result in unfairness to the two players who cooperate to solve the puzzle. I want both of them to try to remember first and then match in an unspeakable tacit understanding, so at last, I keep it.

(You can play this simple game in the top bar named “test” http://rainyiris.cc/test/)

Summary

In this project, I indeed learned a lot about cooperation, puzzle, decoration, and programming.

Thank my wonderful teammates especially Richard. Without him, the project would not turn to be such a good ending. He allows us to talk in Chinese and always give us useful advice. I think that he is kind of person we call “Junzi” in Chinese which means a person of noble character.

I am very sorry that I can’t talk much to him because of my poor English, but I genuinely hope that we can be teammates again in the future.
entry.summary:
O wonder!
How many goodly creatures are there here!
How beauteous mankind is! O brave new world,
That has such people in’t.

— William Shakespeare, The Tempest, Act V, Scene I, ll. 203–206

entry.title: 看见孤独
entry.content:
近些天，突然发觉我能看见孤独。

请不要觉得我脑袋进水了或是中二病又犯了。当然，更严肃且严重一些，的确，有可能是疯了。但即便如此，我还是要说，我，能看见，孤独。

首先，你大概要问，你孤独吗？——还不等我作答，你也许又会说，孤独的话，自然会有这样的错觉。嗯，是吧，可这和个人的状态无关，因为我看见的每个人都是孤独的。你必然不会放过我，接着会紧跟一句，那看见和看不见有区别吗？

先别急，我捏了又捏，和了又和，因为怕这样解释会让你更疑虑，到这里才终于敢讲：我看到的孤独是每个人周身都会散发出的蓝色的光。你肯定不会问话了，因为你笃定我没救了。唉，这时候，我要是照照镜子，自己身上的蓝光恐怕又向外拓了一层。

请容我继续说道些。

那是三天前的事了。没课，到中午才起，准备跑去学校前的餐厅买饭。在卧室外穿鞋的时候瞥见室友门缝底下透出几缕蓝光，没多想也就径直出门了。插一句，英国的晴天其实挺多的，至少我来的这几个月尽是大太阳，偶尔有些云，但很少下雨。那天也是一个晴天。可一走出门就觉得外面的色调有些怪，哪里都泛着蓝，还不匀称。以为是自己宅久了，眼睛里的感光细胞一时适应不了阳光，就边揉眼睛边走。

没见好转，心里慌了起来。走到楼下，一个蓝色的影子从跟前窜了过去，吓了一跳。定睛一看，是个白人熊孩子，小男孩儿，样子挺可爱，就是周身泛着蓝光，实在令人不得不想起俊雄——回头可千万别看见他妈啊，心里如是祈祷着。我别扭地转过身，果然孩子的母亲就在身后——一身亮了许多的蓝光，还好长得和蔼，一脸笑容，不然我可能得叫出声来。我随即打了声招呼，赶忙跑开了。眼睛的问题？那对母子的问题？

我沿着街边的人行道，时闭眼时看脚下走了一会儿，来到了大路上。

我绝望了。满街的人，走在路上的，车窗里的，在脚手架上施工的，在阳台边喝茶的，所有人都毫不留情地，肆无忌惮地炫耀着蓝色的光。

你或许想着可怜可怜我，便问，为什么冒蓝光就是孤独啊？孤独为啥不能是红色的？

说来话长。其实，先前我也完全摸不着头脑，只好憋着不和人提。但我适应得挺快，蓝光而已，没给生活带来什么不便，除了更容易注意到周围的人，有些不自在。正好前两天上课，得和同学老师说话，交流中才发现蓝光是会变化的。比如约了中午一起吃饭，临时有事离开的人蓝光就会亮些，如约一同去的蓝光就会淡些。很自然，我想到了“孤独”。而后就是不断验证是否有反例，结果……这几天来无一例外。所以这才敢说，我能看见孤独。

你仍旧不信？你说退一万步讲，你压根儿不孤独，即便有这样的能力自己也一定不会冒蓝光？

噢，那你一定是世界上最孤单的人吧。
entry.summary: 近些天，突然发觉我能看见孤独。

entry.title: What do we talk about when we talk about board games
entry.content:
Those sentences are printed by mistake because of my roommate. ↑

Then she said, “WOW! I just created art for you!” (๑•́ ∀ •̀๑)

Before 10.10

Someone who saw the title must be thinking this guy should know a lot about board games. However, the truth is that I seldom play board games except for Legends of the Three Kingdoms. Beyond that, I played Armello for 150 hours on steam if it counts and… there is nothing more about board games…

(I hope I could find something to write here.)

Then there are some ideas.

  1. I want to tell a story about space travel.

    A.D. 5000 (or later, whatever) Earth is going to explode because of some unknown reasons (but everyone knows that), so four spaceships are sent to find a new place for we human beings to live. One expected aim is only three constellations away. Then the plot twists:

    Ⅰ When a ship arrives the second constellation, they find some remains of an upper civilization which is the creator of Earth. They get wisdom from that and figure out how to save Earth. So, they just come back.

    Ⅱ A ship locate the planet and successfully slow down and land. However, the astronauts receive the message that it is too late and they shall live alone in the universe as the only group of survivors.

    Ⅲ A ship may find the planet or not, while their speed reducer is broken and they travel too fast at the speed of 99.999% light. Because of the Theory of relativity, they move to the future and are close to the end of the world. They suffer a lot from desperation but finally decide to keep accelerating and fly to another oscillating period of the universe.

I want these three endings or should I say three ways to win.

(Someday, my language teacher asked me “Can a game be literature?” I think this question is quite worth considering. Nowadays many games tell stories, but I don’t think there is one game could compare to Hamlet or Faust. Maybe I can work towards this goal. →So, the first thing is I should rewrite all those !@#$ above.)

  1. Some cards.

    Blocks

    Stars: Spaceships can use their gravity to turn. Their mass differs, so if the speed of a ship is too slow when it approaches a star, it will crash.

    Stardust: Spaceships with a special engine on will accelerate when they cross this area. On the contrary, if the engine is off, spaceships will slow down.

    Empty Space: Just… empty.

    Aim: Remains of upper civilization or Place to live

    Equipment

    Speed Reducer: Slow down to any level.

    “Steering Wheel”: Turn to any direction.

    Accelerator: Accelerate to one higher level.

    Star Slayer: Destroy a star or wipe stardust.

  2. General rules.
    Move spaceships to discover surrounding areas.
    Choose a proper way to win with given items above.

    The Speed of spaceship 1/2/3/4/5
    1 move one step to any direction
    2 move two steps forward
    3 move there steps forward
    4 move four steps forward
    5 can move to a new universe ( but should cross the end of the map)

    constellations Ⅰ/Ⅱ/Ⅲ/Ⅳ
    Ⅰ speed limit ≤ 2 mass of star ① ( if speed ≤ 1 will crash when close to a star)
    Ⅱ speed limit ≤ 3 mass of star ② ( if speed ≤ 2 will crash when close to a star)
    Ⅲ speed limit ≤ 4 mass of star ③ ( if speed ≤ 3 will crash when close to a star)

    All spaceships can discover one step around them and share their maps.

    How to Win
    Reach speed 5 and fly across the end of the map or
    Find Aim at constellations Ⅱ and return to earth and safely land ( speed 1) or
    Find Aim at constellations Ⅲ and safely land ( speed 1)

    10.11

    I am aware that the square shape makes the map too long and players have few choices. So, ↓

After serval tests, I find it’s tough to win by returning to earth. So, I may delete the condition that the spaceship shall safely land (just in this case).

Add explanation of Speed Limit:

You can not accelerate to a higher speed within one constellation.

However, if you turn back with the higher speed, you can still maintain it.

10.12

After the update yesterday, I am very delighted to see… nothing truly changes and still Speed Reducer wins 8 games in 10 no matter how I turn the number of stars and the order of ships.

I may totally change the rules because somehow I can’t keep all these complicated settings balanced.

This reminds me of Dark Souls Series. Miyazaki Hidetaka sometimes sacrifices gameplay for settings like transmission between bonfires(lanterns) in Dark Souls Ⅰ and Bloodborne. However, my tiny little board game can’t bear this kind of sacrifice. (:з」∠)

10.13-10.16

10.13 Play and Think

10.14 Play and Think

10.15 Write and Draw some settings

10.16 Successfully(maybe) change the rules and play a whole day to test if player(s) can have the equal possibility to win

I will introduce the rules later and here is the new story.

Three spaceships are sent to find God or at least prove if God exists, and they have the exact coordinate of where God or miracle is. Like Tower of Babel, this time God prevent we human to reach heaven again. God can ignore physics to change the route of stars and even create new stars. However, at the same time, these actions cannot be detected by humans. So, an invisible war has begun.

In this game, God is much powerful than humans, but dramatically God has to play dice to decide whether or how much he can move or change the stars.

I would like to call this game “If God plays dice.” ( Einstein said “God does not play dice,” but quantum theory… ( ꒪⌓꒪)

10.17-10.18

Now the space travel has become an asymmetrical multiplayer game like Friday the 13th: The Game and Dead by Daylight. Three Spaceships equipped with different items use their unique abilities to travel through the dangerous universe, and God moves or creates stars to prevent them from touching the door of heaven.

(The idea of moving stars came from David.)

I shall keep the rules of speed and movement and set the limitation to 3 for all constellations.

So as the rules of mass, except for slow speed leading to crashing, different stars would interact with each other because of mass and

God can decide whether these interactions happen if they are not detected by spaceships. (This rule makes observation an essential element just like quantum theory.)

A spaceship in my game.

Another one. (that one is too big↑) And this ship is also designed for overlapping.

10.19

I wanted to 3D print those models, but I suddenly find that I have not attended the induction and the nearest one is 10.23… So, I change my mind of making the spaceships and unite them into the same way of shaping stars.

Left part is spaceship which is divided into three parts for a certain height and overlapping. (And the basic shape is like God stretches out his arms to embrace someone.)

Right parts are stars consist of four slices that can be easily combined in three different ways to present different mass.

10.20-10.23

After many tests, rules finally seem to be balanced but still a bit complicated. I will adjust it tomorrow and write a formal version.

Let’s watch some detailed design and artwork first.

Left two are my lovely ships. The right one shows two sides of stars, and you can change the mass of stars by turning the core.

I decide to change previous ships into equipment. Is this a kind of laziness?

Thank Gin for drawing God.

(The board looks too colorful on PC but too dark on paper (≡•̀·̯•́≡)

My first board game is going to finish! (You have to sing this sentence. I am serious.)

Very very very important 10.24

Today my project gets a significant update, and I am delighted with the rules now. Thanks for coming across a game called Santorini that I can finally connect all the elements together and function well and interestingly.

Before the update, there are a few problems in the game:

  1. In the preparing part, God has too much work to place almost all the stars to form the board.
  2. Spaceships have few choices when they are surrounded by stars, and the only two interactions are using an item or crashing on them.
  3. There are few ways for players to interact with each other as well.
  4. Because of the truth above, each turn there is too much calculation but less fun.

So, now rules have been changed like this.↓

Rules

BACKGROUND STORY

In the not too distant future, humans invent an ideal engine bringing incalculable help to space travel. This kind of engine borrows energy from Dirac sea and consumes nothing inversely creating a considerable amount of substances to form new stars. An era of discovering begin. Humans are so ambitious, and they think that they are powerful enough to find God and even reach heaven. So, three spaceships are sent to a precise coordinate called Eden where is very hopeful to witness a miracle. All ships want to be the first bringing valuable report back. However, like the ancient story of the tower of Babel, this time God prevents humans again.

PREPARING TO PLAY

  1. Find 4 people first.

  2. Unfold the board and lay it on the table.

  3. Let the oldest one or the one who looks like the picture of God on the cover be God.

  4. Open the small bag.

    The other three players choose one spaceship and get one controller(a small piece of wood) and a random card with a plug-in component on the back. (You had better not show your component to others.)

    Put the controller on the card at the shape beside Ⅰ.

  5. Put your spaceships at the three yellow hexagons at the bottom of the board (one hexagon for one ship) and let them face vertical to one side of their own hexagon.

    (The left one would be the first to move, so you may fight for this or play dice like God.)

  6. Open the big bag.

    There are stars (a round shape plus a water drop shape) inside. What figure(Ⅰ or Ⅱ or Ⅲ) the drop points decides the mass of the star.

SOME TERMS

Star:

MassⅠ- spaceships cannot step on the hexagon.

MassⅡ- spaceships cannot step on the hexagon and will crash on the star if maintain speedⅠ next turn because of gravity.

MassⅢ- spaceships cannot step on the hexagon and will crash on the star if maintain speedⅡ next turn or pass with speedⅠ this turn because of gravity.

Gravity- at the end of a round,

if a star of massⅠ is close to a star of massⅢ, they will form a new star of massⅡ at the later one’s hexagon.

if a star of massⅠ is close to a star of massⅡ, they will form a new star of massⅢ at the later one’s hexagon.

if a star of massⅡ is close to a star of massⅢ, they will form a new star of massⅠ at the later one’s hexagon.

if all the three conditions happen in a small area, they will happen following the order above and each round will just let one happen.

if the same condition happens in a small area, they will happen as the wish of God.

Miracle Point: The purple hexagon on the top of the board.

Constellation: Three lines of hexagons form a constellation and they have the same color.

Undiscovered:

Stars created by God are put upside down(the side with 11 ovals).

Spaceships can discover their surrounding areas (one step away) at the end of their turn.

Synergism:

When two spaceships stay at the same hexagon, they will face the same direction depending on the lower one. If the speed of the lower one is lower than the upper one, it will carry the upper one to move.

STARTING TO PLAY

Begin with the left player to the middle one then the left then God and repeat each round.

Things for spaceships to do

  1. Choose a speed (place controller at the shape beside Ⅰ or Ⅱ or Ⅲ) and move.
  2. Use a plug-in component.
  3. End their turn to discover the surrounding areas.

Speed:

SpeedⅠ- move one hexagon to any direction vertical to a side. (before the end of your turn you can spin thousands of degrees as you like.)

SpeedⅡ- move two hexagons with maximum one turning vertical to an adjacent side. (You can turn when you move to the second hexagons.) After movement, create a star of massⅠ put it on a hexagon surround.

You shall not create a star when you enter or move out the miracle point.

SpeedⅢ- move three hexagons vertical to your current facing side. After movement, create a star of massⅡ put it on a hexagon surround.

You shall not create a star when you enter or move out of the miracle point.

(Attention- you cannot speed up from Ⅰ to Ⅲ or slow down from Ⅲ to Ⅰ)

Crash & Reborn:

Crash on the edge- reborn at one of the three beginning hexagons. After you reach the miracle point, reborn there. Each spaceship can only reborn once in each condition.

Crash on a star- follow the rules of Speed and the star will reduce 1 level of mass. (Star of MassⅠ will be wiped.) The same reborn setting.

plug-in component:

Warp Accelerator- jump over a star or a spaceship in front of your facing hexagon.

Star Slayer- wipe a star or reduce it to a level of mass (if the star is still undiscovered, you can tell other player reduce 1 level or 2 levels or 3 levels but not a certain mass.) within two constellations.

Space Folder- push anything on your facing hexagon one step away, and if there is another star or spaceship on the remote hexagon, both will be destroyed.

All plug-in component can be used once, but reborn or reach the miracle point can refresh them.

Things for God to do

  1. Play dice.

    Dice: A four-sided one. The upper corner shows the result Z.

  2. Create an undiscovered star of massX in the middle two constellations (two kinds of green colors).

    Move a star of massY to a hexagon one step away from its former hexagon.

    X+Y≤Z

    Both two actions cannot be seen (one step away) by spaceships and can just be done once in a round.

  3. Decide the effects between stars due to gravity at the end of the round.

TO WIN

The first spaceship getting to the purple hexagon and returning back to one of the three beginning hexagons wins.

If no spaceship can make the journey, God wins.

I will explain how these rules and settings work and why I choose them.

10.25

It is definitely a lovely shiny day in my life. I have to say that Alan’s class is very very very… (there are maybe thousands of very here) interesting and helpful. What’s more, I feel deeply happy when I play my poor game with classmates and… miss the lunch.

I may summarize this project after finishing the report. ( The picture below is kind of like the report, infinite and profound.)

10.26
entry.summary:
Those sentences are printed by mistake because of my roommate. ↑

Then she said, “WOW! I just created art for you!” (๑•́ ∀ •̀๑)

entry.title: 狗
entry.content:
晨光透过百叶窗的缝隙筛在陈平脸上，本就有些臃肿的两颊生生地被固定在光与影的夹缝里。有光的一道道白条处，每个毛孔都显得格外清楚，像是在贪婪地吮吸着什么，肉也好像要满溢出来。有可能是在梦里看到了自己这副丑态，陈平立即翻了个身，脸朝下蒙在枕头里睡去了。

“嗡嗡”，枕边的手机响了——这不是什么新鲜的事，从微博到浏览器，陈平手机里每个软件都会这样殷勤地推送着许多或这里或那里产生的信息，可没有一条是只给他的。“嗡嗡”、“嗡嗡”、“嗡嗡”……情况有些失控，难道，是世界末日了。陈平一下子得出了这个结论，并为此沾沾自喜。“嗡嗡”、“嗡嗡”、“嗡嗡”……一点都没有停的意思，他几乎可以笃定这个无可救药的世界就将在今天，今天这样一个再普通不过的周日画上句号。为了贯彻一如既往的严谨精神，他终于拿起手机来确认，并想要写一首关于末日与肥胖的诗，留做纪念。

“狗死了”“我家的狗”“就是那只蠢狗啊”“活不下去了”……赫然出现在屏幕上的是这些话。再看到名字，是陈平“前一任”女朋友——这样的描述让他自己也觉得既奇妙又悲伤，奇妙在，一个“肥宅”竟也和好些个女孩子交往过，悲伤则溢于言表了。陈平盯着屏幕看了好久，愣是看不出个所以然来——字缝里既没有财色也没有吃人——只是心中五味杂陈，甚至胃里也是。他踉跄着进了厕所，冲着马桶吐了起来。闭着眼睛吐完，他赶忙按下了冲水键，想到这些天吃的都是咖喱，又有些犯恶心。

“狗”这个字陈平并不陌生，任谁也不陌生吧。只是此刻“狗”这个概念像是刚登陆地球的外星舰队一样，奇异而庞大。他以前是不喜欢狗的，毕竟狗也不喜欢他。

小学时，一天放学，陈平一手持伞模仿动画里忍者跑步姿势开心地往家进发。这时，身后同样传来了密集的脚步声，说时迟那时快，他一个转身顺势把伞挥了出去，伞尖凌厉地打中了什么。等自己回过神来，视野里只剩下远处落荒而逃的背影。没错，那是一只黄色的狗——应该就是从那时起，陈平就成了狗的敌人。

不知是爱屋及乌，还是单纯讨好，自从和那女孩子交往以来，陈平开始喜欢起狗来，至少是装作喜欢起来。他也分不清这两者的差别。他会买火腿肠喂流浪狗，会向路边经过的狗投去微笑，甚至亲自动手帮女友做了狗窝——一只黄色的狗的狗窝。

“死”对陈平而言更是再熟悉不过了。他无数次想过自杀，还一直在努力设计漂亮的自杀方式，比如密室——用机械装置控制门和刀，或者把床升到半空，或者设置定时的毒气弹。陈平对他的创意每每都能感到背脊一凉，还有说不出的孤独，但就是这种感觉让陈平觉得自己真实地活着。诚然，他不确定自己是否真的想死，只是这种想法实在太迷人了。

原来是狗死了，陈平这才意识到这件事和世界末日的区别。它无非是件无论何时无论何地，发生了无数次的，和他的名字一样稀松平常的事。他和女友的相识相知相处到离别又何尝不是这样一件事呢，这世界上无时无刻不在上演这样的故事。如果上帝被要求每天坐在电视前看他的造物历经的那些悲欢离合，亿万年过去了，恐怕也成了难耐的煎熬吧。

那只狗叫什么来着，欢欢？乐乐？可可？总之是叠词。陈平曾经把那只狗视作自己最美好最纯洁的感情的象征，因为在狗身上并没有肉欲，而自己不计成本的付出又是实实在在的。可如今，却连名字也忘记了。

陈平又打开手机，前女友的信息像是疯长的爬山虎一样爬了满屏，但总结起来还是“狗死了”。这让他想到之前两人上万页的聊天记录，总结起来也不过就是“我爱你”和“我不爱你了”。

更可悲的是，陈平并不知道自己是否真的爱过她。就像他不知道自己是不是真的变得喜欢狗了一样。但只要假设他爱过，那现在他就能确定自己还爱着——所以，那用几千页的言语表达“我不爱你了”的人又是谁呢？陈平不知道，因为他连着狗的名字把自己一并忘记了。他背井离乡，甚至远赴重洋，就是为了忘记——忘记一切自己做过的，曾经想做的，后来没做的事——但哪里会有一片没有狗的乐土呢。

陈平反复摩擦着牙齿，拼命敲出了一个“哦”字。然后是点下发送，他这样想。不，我不能，他转念又这样想。大脑经过无数次斗争，额头上都渗出了汗。而好不容易，前一种想法占了上风，陈平的手却不听使唤地点到了Home键。不大的屏幕上出现了一只黄色的狗

——原来，它一直就是壁纸啊。

陈平恍然大悟，把手机丢到一旁，栽倒在枕头上，一如起床之前。

晨光透过百叶窗的缝隙筛在陈平后脑勺上，本就有些稀疏的头发生生地被固定在光与影的夹缝里。有光的一道道白条处，每个毛孔都显得格外清楚，像是在贪婪地吮吸着什么，肉也好像要满溢出来。即便在梦里看到了自己这副丑态，陈平却再也不愿也不能反抗了。
entry.summary: 晨光透过百叶窗的缝隙筛在陈平脸上，本就有些臃肿的两颊生生地被固定在光与影的夹缝里。有光的一道道白条处，每个毛孔都显得格外清楚，像是在贪婪地吮吸着什么，肉也好像要满溢出来。有可能是在梦里看到了自己这副丑态，陈平立即翻了个身，脸朝下蒙在枕头里睡去了。

entry.title: Falling London
entry.content:
It’s a journal for ‘The London Project’.

Picture from a webpage game called ‘Fallen London’ ↑

Both trapped on the Internet and in the real world

When I was at home in China, I used to rent a sever in London to build something programers called “ladder“ so that I could access to websites like Facebook, instagram, pinterest, etc.

Here in London, I thought I could surf the Internet freely. However, I am still blocked by other “walls“ and have to rent a sever back home to access to Youku, bilibili, QQmusic, etc.

On the shelf

I almost fell in love with them at first sight granted that they were just common ikea lamps.

Is love actually here?

This street reminded me of one of my favorite movies called “Love actually“.

But I am not sure whether it was filmed there. Emmmmm… because I was actually lost.

Two victorian things: comeback of Gothic & Steampunk

Victorian era is really cool!!! Then someone told me that “Bloodborne“ is full of Georgian things.

Well, Georgian era is really … so what’s the difference?

Déjà vu

Did the sculpture of Anthony Gormley stand on that building?

Dream & Reality

Much better ↓

Burst agonized and clear!

Great machine comparing to PowerPoint.

Distance

By the way, the book was first published in 2017.

Distance again

I am here in London GMT +1 because of summer time.

One of my best friends studies fine art in Tokyo GMT +9.

Another best friend in Seattle majors in CS also summer time GMT -7.

Generally we divide the world into natural thirds.

Yesterday

Thank god Paul is a genius!

Dancing Man

It means “N” in Sir Conan Doyle’s novel.

Aha!

Good afternoon, president.

Little inspiration

Cities are full of grey. Green is a present from nature, while red can start a flame to light all in heaven and earth.

Omelette

Faces

Human plus penguin! n(≧▽≦)n

Wings

Wings, wings, black and white. Why both of you are flying on the ground?

Island & Isolation

‘No Man is an Island’
No man is an island entire of itself; every man
is a piece of the continent, a part of the main;
if a clod be washed away by the sea, Europe
is the less, as well as if a promontory were, as
well as any manner of thy friends or of thine
own were; any man’s death diminishes me,
because I am involved in mankind.
And therefore never send to know for whom
the bell tolls; it tolls for thee.

——John Donne

I used to be on a continent and I am now on a true island.

However, I feel the same kind of isolation as usual.
entry.summary:
It’s a journal for ‘The London Project’.

Picture from a webpage game called ‘Fallen London’ ↑

entry.title: 致亲爱的母亲
entry.content:
今天是母亲节。三天前，谋划着做个小动画给您看看。转眼三天过去了，却没能如自己所想拿出礼物。您的儿子确实有些懒呢。除了懒，还不坦诚，不上进，有千个万个小毛病，千个万个不好——但有一点好，他真的很爱你啊！

回想起那些黏在你屁股后面的日子，有点不好意思，又着实感觉幸福。闭上眼，那些琐碎的记忆涌上心头，即便是最最微不足道的片段，也是最醇的酒，最甜的蜜。

  

大概是上幼儿园的时候。有一次，外面飘着雨，雨点打窗上“咚咚咚”响个不停。您在厨房剥花生，我怯生生地走过来，问：“今天，我不用去上学吗？”您看了看窗外的雨，摸摸我的头，说：“雨太大了啊。来，帮我剥花生。”说着递了个小板凳给我。突如其来的喜讯让我兴奋了好久，剥起花生来格外卖力。直到近两年，看您给小锴讲假日安排的时候，才猛然发觉——那天大概本来就不用上课吧！

嗯，您还真是狡猾呢！

  

到了小学。四年级的时候，因为看不清黑板上的字，您带我去查视力。一下子就是400度近视，您特别忧虑，给我配了眼镜不说，还试了各种偏方，“捏耳朵”啊，“眼保仪”啊。工作再忙，您总能抽出时间来载我去“看眼睛”。平日的督促也从未间断。钱销去不少，却收效甚微。那时候，我特别惭愧，感觉自己给家里添了许多麻烦。其实，我挺喜欢眼镜的。

唉，没办法，您总是这样为我操心。

  

初中发生了许多事。大事小事，堆积如山。我记得最清楚的却是有天晚上您教我做一道数学题。那时，我还是对自己的数学水平很有自信的。结果，那晚扑到一个“难”题上，愣头愣脑算了半天却解不出来，颇为恼火。是您，用非常简单实用的思路一下子算了出来，让我佩服了许久。

所以，我心底里觉得您很聪明啊，应该更挺起胸脯才是！

  

相较初中，高中稀松平常了许多。要说波澜，肯定要属我离家出走那次。那天回到家，坐在二楼客厅的沙发上，您问我：“是不想读书了吗？”我畏畏缩缩了半天，挤出来半个“是”。您谈了许多，从您自己谈到了我，谈到了我们，谈到了家。第二天，我还是回学校了。而后，虽然有些波折，但最终我读完了高中，上了大学。

无疑，您是我人生的灯塔。此刻，我仍不知道该航向何方，可回头看到您的光亮，心中便少了许多迷惘。

  

大学这四年，在家的时间一下子少了许多。层层叠叠堆在心里的话，我也总是不好意思表达。通电话大半是家里工作上的事——其实我嘴上说麻烦，心里却非常非常喜欢帮忙画图。因为，总觉得多少能帮上些忙，多少能填补一点我的亏欠。

这几次考雅思，成绩不理想。我和初中配眼镜时一样愧疚，但却总是提不起劲来好好备考。我时常在想，自己的记忆和反应明明不输别人，甚至有过人的地方。这些都是您给我的天赋，我却不懂好好运用。我总是在逃避。辜负了您对我的信任。我理应做得更好更好。

  

我知道，我有个出色的母亲。我总能在人前夸耀母亲是个温柔而又坚强的人。我也多么希望自己能变得像你一样。

我也总和人提起您唱歌非常好听。如果有人问我是怎样出生的，我会说：“我的母亲是司掌音乐的女神。她唱起歌来比一万只百灵鸟齐声还要宛转悠扬！生命之树在她耳畔低语：‘你若是能让世间所有生灵都哭泣，你的孩子便会获得祝福而降生在这世上’。于是她在清晨放声歌唱，万物都为之感动流泪，这便是晨露。而后就有了我。”

  

因为您的出色，我也会反思自己的坏性格。反思自己是不是个好儿子。对小锴而言是不是个好哥哥。陪他搭积木、教他做作业的时候，自己的不耐烦有没有吓到他。无心说出来的粗言秽语，有没有对他造成影响。

我有时庆幸，自己能有比较开阔的视野看问题，思考问题，能理解哲学，能欣赏音乐和电影，能发现事物和事物间稍深层一些的联系；有时又觉得，要是能更单纯地看待生活中的一切该多好。

最近听您说小锴要上很多兴趣班，每周末都要风尘仆仆地赶到这儿赶到那儿。

我想说，这些还是要以小锴是否快乐为判断标准。电子琴课要让他觉得音乐是快乐的，画画课要让他觉得美术是快乐的……因为有这些快乐在，无论理解与否，生命才愈加有意义。

  

上次您来学校，问我的那些话，我还记得——那时扭扭捏捏，不愿讲，又讲不清。现在想想真是莫名其妙啊，我明明知道的，我知道而且应该毫不吝啬地从心底大声呼喊：

若这世上所有感情皆是虚妄，有且仅有一份是真实的，那就是您给我的爱。如果有两份的话，那就再加上我对您的爱。妈，我爱你！

  

您不肖的儿子

2018年5月13日
entry.summary: 今天是母亲节。三天前，谋划着做个小动画给您看看。转眼三天过去了，却没能如自己所想拿出礼物。您的儿子确实有些懒呢。除了懒，还不坦诚，不上进，有千个万个小毛病，千个万个不好——但有一点好，他真的很爱你啊！

entry.title: 米洛斯的天使
entry.content:
“Heaven on the earth. Angel in the sky. Kings never rise, oracles ever died, where Milos lies.”车队隆隆地向着米洛斯前进，吟游诗人倚着马车的栏杆，扶了扶竹编的三角帽，望向远方依稀可见的城镇和盘旋在城镇上空的“天使”。

一

米洛斯城位于大陆南端广袤沙漠的中心，被零星几片绿洲环绕，是众多横贯沙漠的商路上重要的中转站。米洛斯富饶而美丽，城内密布着大大小小的教堂，宽敞的街巷挤满了来自大陆各地的商旅，广场上哲学家、诗人和孩子们齐聚一堂……自古以来，人们就对米洛斯充满向往，可谓奇迹之地——水源充沛、粮食充足，财富和珍宝从四面八方滚滚而来，知识和文化在这里碰撞出灿烂的火花。

这里，没有天灾，甚至没有人祸——这或许都要归功于天空中那只巨大的有翼生物，“天使”。

“天使”本应是仅存在于人类幻想中的神之使者，此刻却无比真实地飞翔在米洛斯上空——天使会喃喃地祈祷，会落下纯白的羽毛，并在外邦人触怒神时降下天罚——一道白色光线，炽热而致命。米洛斯人称之为“朗基努斯之枪”，它会准确地贯穿罪人的躯体，一触即亡，只留下一个小小的圆形开口，生命好像就从那里漏了出来。“罪人”的定义十分宽泛，有任何直接或间接（如纵火、投毒等）加害他人的想法足矣。“而神是善的，他希望人悬崖勒马（米洛斯第29任主教如是写道）”，所以直到罪人动手那一瞬，“朗基努斯之枪”才会发动。关于“罪人”，学界一直存在着一个有趣的争论，一部分学者认为“米洛斯人生来受到天使的偏爱，即便犯罪也不会受到天使的惩罚”，反对者称“米洛斯人只是一直生活在没有‘犯罪’的环境中，犯罪率极低，而米洛斯雪藏了那些罪案”。

米洛斯人世世代代生活在天使的庇佑下。不论沙漠内外的国度怎样变迁，王朝如何更迭，米洛斯都从未对任何势力俯首称臣。所以即便在这个战火纷飞、人命如草芥的时代，每年都有数以万计的人历经沙漠恶劣气候和猖獗盗匪的重重考验最终抵达米洛斯。其中，商人最多，其次是朝圣者，当然还有一些可怜的难民。

诗人名叫“洛”，从遥远的北方国度来，身上有着1/4米洛斯血统。洛的祖父来自米洛斯，是个怪人，心地善良却总是摆着一张臭脸。祖父非常了解米洛斯和沙漠里各个国家的历史，却从来不愿提及自己为何离开那里。而洛童年时就向往着这座天使之城，醉心于阅读描写米洛斯的故事，听祖父讲那远方沙漠里的英雄事迹以及神的恩惠与责罚。终于，在20岁生日那天，洛受当地商贾资助，踏上了前往米洛斯的朝圣之旅。他想创作一组记录旅途中遭遇和米洛斯神迹的长诗带回家乡。

车队渐渐行进到了米洛斯城外的街市，这里主要聚集了实力不济的小商队和大部分难民，治安有些混乱。再往前走，就到了一处规整的环形缓冲区，环宽为300米，夹在“朗基努斯之枪”的最大射程和米洛斯城墙之间。各国的学者、外交官、驻派的小规模部队大都停驻在这里。有人认为在这里驻派部队是愚昧之举，因为士兵在这里发挥不了任何作用——而且或许是好斗的天性使然，每年总有几名喝醉了的士兵死于天使的长枪。缓冲区是为了防止有人在天使的保护范围外，用弓弩狙击城内的市民或妄图用投石车破坏建筑。不过，随着射石炮类的大型火器出现，不断有市民提出在城墙内再修建一道内墙。

商队终于行至米洛斯城墙下。首先要经过简单的排查，然后是清点商品的数量，评估价值，确定一行人可以在城内逗留的时间。洛有了在米洛斯经商多年的商队头领担保，获准在城里的商人公会住上一个月。

据说，百余年前，也就是“艾尔”，离统一沙漠诸邦仅有一步之遥的英雄王，在此处殒命之时，米洛斯的戒备远比现如今森严得多，入城者要经过繁琐而冗长的层层检查。学者称那时的米洛斯仍有一定数量的常备军，都是身披重甲，手持巨盾的死士，他们往往据守一夫当关万夫莫开的要道，有时也须冲锋陷阵，但都是为了尽力拖延敌人的步伐。因为只要时间足够，所有入侵者都会被天使剿灭。然而百年前的那场战事却意外平静，艾尔的大军中甚至没有一人能碰到米洛斯的城墙。包括米洛斯人在内，全世界都重新评估了天使的力量，各国都先后与米洛斯签订了永久和平协定。这之后，常备军也随之解散。

洛下了马车，开始步行，缓缓穿过了米洛斯不算高的城门。映入眼帘的是层层叠叠铺展开去的建筑和天空中那只巨大的天使。米洛斯的建筑群呈圆锥型，由城墙向城中心渐渐升高。至高处耸立着一座圆顶的塔，名为“阿瓦”，位于米洛斯最宏伟的大教堂花园内。传说，塔顶种着一株樱桃树，是米洛斯永世不衰的象征。

洛此行最大的愿望就是能一睹那株樱桃树的真容。为此，必须获得大主教的批准。

天色已晚，洛在公会安顿好后，努力平复内心的激动之情，推开了自己房间的窗。窗外是一轮皎洁的明月和迷一般静静飞翔的天使，时不时飘落的羽毛在月光下闪耀着银辉，像轻柔的雪，又像剑的寒芒。洛看得入了神，殊不知有一片羽毛缓缓飘到他的头上。轻柔的触感让洛想起了母亲的手，思绪转而飞向了远方的家乡。可那羽毛是虚妄之物，随后便化作点点荧光消散了。

二

第二日，洛换上家乡的正装，一早就前往教堂。除了城内的几处广场，清晨的米洛斯静得出奇，洛总觉得自己的脚步声有些突兀，好像会妨碍到街边商贩的耳语。路上，许多人向这个异邦小伙子投来或吃惊或赞许的目光。想来原因也简单，一是洛本就长得俊俏，气宇不凡，二是非米洛斯本地的年轻人如此昂首阔步地走在大街上是十分少见的，三是洛腰间竟束着一柄装饰用的短刀。外乡人在米洛斯似乎总是卑躬屈膝的，生怕遭受天罚。更不用说，年轻是易燃的柴薪，一点火光都会连同生命一起燃烧殆尽。洛这样大方磊落的举止在大多数人看来就是玩火自焚吧。

行至教堂门外，绘有装饰画的巨大玻璃窗和镂满金丝花纹的大门令洛颇为惊叹，然而教堂内祷告的人却寥寥无几，大概是大教堂附近没有民居区而米洛斯有太多教堂的缘故。毕竟米洛斯人的信仰是虔诚的，而绕远路对于虔诚的信仰是没有助益的。

洛进了教堂，在长椅上等候。大主教这时正在聆听信徒的忏悔。洛打算借此机会和他谈一谈，获得登上“阿瓦”的许可。这时，从忏悔室走出了一位身着红色长裙，脚踩木屐的年轻女孩子。所有人的目光一下子都集中在这个正快步离开教堂的女孩子身上，倒不是因为她生得美丽，而是她手里紧握着一把亮闪闪的剪刀。在米洛斯城正中，天使的眼皮子底下手持锐器，更不知需要多大的勇气和自信。

女孩裹着米色头巾，五官小巧精致，神情有股咄咄逼人的气势。从洛身边经过时，他发现女孩握着剪刀的手细小却满是老茧，难道是个裁缝？正当洛这样猜想时，一位年轻的教士走了出来，宣布：“大主教身体不适，后面就由我为大家答疑解惑，我会尽我所能……”在场的其他人似乎并不在意这件事，只有洛无奈起身离开。

教堂外，女孩还未走远。洛追了上去，“你是米洛斯人吧。”

这话像是石子投进了干涸的深井，只传回了撞击井壁的“咚咚”声，女孩儿接着向前走着。

“我听说洛斯人一般从事神职或是蓄养牲畜，‘天职’（即职业）从父辈那里继承，很少有人更换，即便更换也会选择公务人员。裁缝这种高危职业，都是交给外乡人做的吧。”

石子似乎落弹到了井壁的凹陷处，划出一个奇怪的弧线，高高跃起，一时没了声响。女孩儿停住了脚步，似乎想说的点什么，后又头也不回地继续前进，“咚、咚、咚”。

“也许有些冒昧，但我觉得你有点像我的母亲……当然，我不是说年龄！——应该是气质一类的东西…？”

石子越落越深，声响却因回音越来越大，沉重而略带愠怒，“咚——咚——咚”。

“如果可以的话，做我的导游吧。我从小就很喜欢这座城……”

从井底传来了可怕的呼嚎，声场把石子反向吹了出来，直至井口。

女孩突然转身，“无知的外乡人！离我远点！”声音因激动而有些颤抖。

“我没有恶意，只是我向往这里，而或许你对外面的世界感兴趣，我们可以各取所需。”

石子忽地从井口迅捷地下坠，越落越快，终于轻灵入水。

“那……你能带我出城吗？”

“什么？”洛感受到从身后上方刮来的风，迟疑了一下，“你认真的吗？”

“嗯，我想出城——在这城里，只要你不想死，什么事我都能帮你。想死的话，天上那东西就行。”女孩凑了过来，胸前的剪刀微微前倾，正对着洛的心脏。洛没有动，只是静静地看着女孩儿，她的眼睛像极了母亲，或者说更像外祖父。

“我叫莉莉丝，请多指教。”

三

太阳西沉，依旧澄澈的天空出现了月影。熙熙攘攘的人群徘徊在街道上，没有散去的意思。这时的米洛斯比白天更静，好像人们只是低着头漫无目的地踱着，灵魂早已不安分地离开躯体，向着天空飞去了。天使展开双翅，迎接那些上升的思绪，把它们捧向天堂。

莉莉丝和洛一路走到了城外延的小集市。洛显得有些兴奋，看这看那，像个不知疲倦的孩子。莉莉丝则显得有些不耐烦，时不时跺跺脚催促着。这片集市是外乡人最集中的地方之一，主要经营一些稀奇古怪的小玩意儿——刻着神秘图腾的挂饰啊，印着青色花纹的瓶瓶罐罐啊，传言可以控制动物的乐器或是驱魔的武器……

洛来到一家门面中等大小的饰品店前，稍驻足，点头确认了一下，便径直朝店内走去。莉莉丝紧随其后，发现店里从地面到天花板铺满了闪闪发光的水晶和玻璃制品，越往里走堆得越密，最后仅容下寥寥几处落脚的空地。屋内靠墙正中摆着一张木桌，桌上摆满了鉴赏珠宝和有关玻璃制作的书籍，有多种字母文字，甚至有米洛斯文（一种由画给神的祈祷图案发展来的象形文字）译本。一位头发斑白的老人从书堆里探出头来，推了下老花镜，凑近看了看洛，缩回脖子瞥了一眼莉莉丝，又埋头翻起书来。

未完待续
entry.summary: “Heaven on the earth. Angel in the sky. Kings never rise, oracles ever died, where Milos lies.”车队隆隆地向着米洛斯前进，吟游诗人倚着马车的栏杆，扶了扶竹编的三角帽，望向远方依稀可见的城镇和盘旋在城镇上空的“天使”。

entry.title: 兔子
entry.content:
很久很久以前，有一座宁静平和的小镇依山而建。镇中心有个圆形广场，广场中心是一座喷泉，喷泉中心则雕刻着一只神态端庄的短毛猫——这只猫注视着的广场和由广场通向镇上的大小街道，是受商旅们眷顾的地方。每到礼拜日，就会有成群结队的商人远道而来，让这个略显偏僻的小镇热闹非凡。

小镇上有位青年，靠卖气球为生，常和一只黑毛兔子为伴，大家都称他为“兔子杰克”。杰克生在一个有着悠久历史和光荣传统的商贾家庭，然而这些都已不复当年。杰克的祖辈曾经拥有当地最大的橡胶林，富甲一方。而父辈的挥霍，橡胶的贬值，和一场当地人全都不愿提及的悲剧，让可怜的杰克在很小的时候就成了孤儿，只能贱卖所剩不多的家产，勉强度日。幸运的是，杰克为自己保住了一小片橡胶林，并拾起了原先家里副业中的副业，做起了气球生意。

每周末，杰克都会去镇中心的广场。来往的商人们都很乐意为自己的孩子买上几只气球。孩子们总会拿着气球开心地绕着喷泉疯跑，像颠簸的马车上糖果盒子里的彩色糖果，不时地碰在一起，盒子里就弥漫着糖粉——整个广场的空气也因此变得欢快而甜蜜——不仅如此，杰克还能见到自己的心上人，艾米莉。

艾米莉的父亲是这个小镇的领主，一位深居简出的伯爵。艾米莉则活泼了许多，喜欢旅行和打猎，还常在礼拜日去镇中心的泉水边喂鸽子。她总会向杰克买一只红色的气球，红得像递过气球时杰克的脸颊。

杰克第一次去广场上卖气球的时候，也是第一次和艾米莉相遇的时候。艾米莉在喷泉的水池里救起了一只奄奄一息的黑兔子。痴痴地盯着艾米莉的杰克立刻上前帮忙，还好心收养了它，起名为沃格。有些神奇的是，沃格长着一对炯炯有神的红眼睛，而且十分机灵，完全不用杰克费心照顾。自从与沃格相遇，杰克的运气也似乎好了许多，气球生意蒸蒸日上。于是，杰克总把它带在身边。一人一兔，朝夕相伴，形影不离。

其实，艾米莉也对杰克芳心暗许，但两人却未能互诉衷肠。因为这段姻缘注定不会得到伯爵的祝福，而他掌控着这座小镇的生杀予夺，更牢牢把握着女儿的命运。

一年万圣节，按照惯例，伯爵举办了盛大的假面舞会，邀请领地里的地主乡绅参加，并为女儿物色如意郎君。听说今年还邀请到了一位有王位继承权的公爵。那天夜里，杰克第一次去艾米莉的住处送装饰舞会用的气球。富丽堂皇的伯爵宅邸令杰克叹为观止，厅堂中心的吊灯仿佛点缀着漫天的繁星，脚下的丝绒地毯像是被太阳晒了一天的软草皮，窗台的装饰则宛若沿着草地铺展到天边的鲜花……这些让杰克想起了幼年时在相似的场景里蹒跚学步，想起了衣食无忧的生活，想起了自己本不该如此不堪的命运——杰克想“回家”，而艾米莉显然是一条捷径。

送完气球的杰克并没有就此打道回府，而是偷偷地在宅邸里找寻艾米莉的踪影。兜兜转转间，杰克迷了路，只能失意地坐在路旁的石凳上，等着被护卫撵出去。这时藏在怀里的沃格突然不安分地钻了出来，机灵地动了动耳朵，用浑圆的眼睛盯着杰克——沃格突然咧开嘴，笑了起来，把杰克吓得向后一退，更令杰克吃惊的是，沃格竟像模像样地开口讲起话来：“噢噢噢，我亲爱的主人！不要惊慌！现在我要正式介绍一下自己！在下名为沃格，曾经是个远近闻名的炼金术士。啊，当然是个人类炼金术士，因为一点小事故，如你所见，变成了现在这副样子。即便如此，我的知识和力量没有完全消失，比如在你给我起名字时，就稍稍动用了一下，再比如我可以告诉你艾米莉在哪里……”“艾米莉在哪里？”听到艾米莉的名字，杰克一瞬间忘记了恐惧。沃格的眼珠轱辘一转，眼睛忽的眯成一条缝，继续挂着夸张的笑说道：“我做炼金术士时，总是贪婪地向别人、向自然索取，如今却落魄至此。是你和艾米莉救了我，到我回报的时候了。你沿着这条路走下去，路左边能看到大厅外连着楼梯的小平台，在正中间的平台等着就行了。另外，勇敢地去追求就行，记住，我会帮忙的。”

杰克照做了。果不其然，没过多久，艾米莉就叹着气从偏门走了出来。杰克刚想上前，却发现她身后跟着神情有些气愤的伯爵。伯爵先看见了杰克，冷冷地喊了一句：“你小子怎么还在这里！”杰克胆怯了，想扭头就走，双脚却忽然不听使唤，反而冒出来一句：“我想娶您的女儿！”伯爵、艾米莉乃至杰克自己都吃了一惊，而后，艾米莉露出了淡淡的微笑，伯爵则是一阵冷笑：“哼！你！就你！凭什么娶我的女儿？”“我可以从贪婪的巨龙口中夺取世界上最大最美的红宝石项链，献给您的女儿，证明我的勇敢、力量和对您女儿无限的爱慕。”杰克看似回答得沉着而激昂，心里却满是担忧，他不懂这番由沃格替他说出来的话，有几分可信。伯爵被杰克一反常态的气势震住了几秒，又恢复了之前的戏谑：“好啊，很好很好！只要你拿得出那样的项链，我就把女儿嫁给你！本伯爵可不能亏待屠龙的英雄嘛！”“那我这就出发，等着我的好消息吧。”杰克深情地望了一眼怔在一旁的艾米莉，转身阔步离去了。

回到住处，杰克又喜又急，不知该如何是好。沃格则不紧不慢地从杰克的衣襟钻了出来，跳到屋里的小圆桌中心坐定，咧开嘴笑道：“剜掉我的右眼！”杰克一愣。沃格继续说：“剜掉我的右眼。它里面藏着巨龙占有的那颗红宝石。我年轻时随着师傅学习炼金术，一本师傅视为至宝的炼金典籍里，写着‘巨龙的鳞片使巨龙不朽’，于是师傅便带着我去遥远的极北屠龙。巨龙陨落，师傅对龙鳞以外的东西毫无兴趣，于是我就取走了巨龙口中的红宝石项链。现在它藏在我的右眼里。”沃格见杰克不愿动手，补充说：“我变成兔子以后，寿命也短得可怜。我已经时日无多了，右眼你就拿去吧。”杰克踌躇了许久，不忍心下手。于是，沃格又说：“今天的晚会上，公爵向艾米莉示爱。虽然艾米莉婉言拒绝，但恐怕伯爵是不会拒绝的……”

随着几声雷鸣，窗外突然下起了雨。淅淅沥沥，淅淅沥沥，侧耳倾听，雨声中好像夹杂着几声呜咽。

“兔子杰克出发屠龙”的消息不胫而走。几天后，一个晴朗的早晨，杰克以一身探险家的装束出现在了伯爵的宅邸前，腰间束着一柄阔剑，手持一个小布袋。“杰克屠龙归来了？”“那柄剑能刺透龙鳞？”“手里是传说中的红宝石项链吗？”“傻子，那是布袋。”路人们议论纷纷，奔走相告，不一会儿伯爵的宅邸前就被围得水泄不通。

伯爵和艾米莉从宅子里走了出来。还没等伯爵发话，杰克就解开了布袋。先是一道红光从缝隙里刺了出来，然后光芒溢满了整个天空，太阳黯然失色，时间仿佛也凝滞了。半晌无言，红光突然收束了回来，像是顺便吸走了阳光和目光，天空暗淡了许多，人们也都木然地盯着宝石。杰克率先打破了沉默，走上前去为艾米莉戴上了项链。艾米莉也拥住了杰克，伯爵没有阻拦。随后，人群中响起了一浪高过一浪的掌声和欢呼声。

杰克作为屠龙英雄被邀请到伯爵的宅邸接受款待，并会在七天后与艾米莉正式成婚，然后可能永远在伯爵家住下，甚至继承爵位。然而，艾米莉的样子有些奇怪。她一连好几天都把自己锁在屋里，对打猎和旅行都提不起兴致，甚至对杰克也冷冰冰的，但脸上却总挂着无忧无虑的微笑，还时不时望着远方的虚空，好像尘世间再没有什么值得她留恋了。

杰克很担心艾米莉，打算向她问清楚情况。独眼的沃格突然从杰克的袖口挤了出来，有些疲惫地顺着衣袖爬上了杰克的肩膀，在他耳边低语：“剜去我的左眼！艾米莉对你不理不睬，是因为她获得了永生，那条项链才是巨龙不朽的秘密。她透过蕴藏无限生命的红宝石看清了世间的生死轮回，她在意的人、爱的人都会离她而去，包括你。你要想和她长相厮守，得拥有和她对等的力量。”沃格气喘吁吁，停了一会儿，接着说：“我的左眼里有遥远南方的王者生前佩戴的红宝石戒指。师傅研究龙鳞无果，郁郁而终。那时我已到中年，决定动身前往大陆的最南端游历，那里广袤的土地上只有一个共同的王。他南征北伐，攻无不克，战无不胜，天下归心，依靠的就是这枚戒指。后来突如其来的沙尘暴淹没了王都，国王也难逃一劫。我在王都的遗址里找到了它。剜去我的左眼！戒指和项链是一对，它们靠近的时候会相互呼应，你和艾米莉就能获得永远的生命和无上的权力。”沃格怕杰克不肯下手，便又补充：“你看看我的现在样子。我的生命是靠项链和戒指维系的，缺一不可。项链已经不属于我，留着戒指也于事无补。你拿去吧，这样就可以报答你们的救命之恩了……”沃格说着说着没了力气，眼看要倒在杰克肩上——忽然又一跃而起，落到了杰克身旁的窗沿上，堆起了夸张的笑，说：“还有一顶镶嵌着红宝石的王冠就藏在广场喷泉的猫塑像里，它可以给人带来无尽的财富，需要的时候就去取吧。”

一声惊雷，响彻云霄。大雨倾盆而下，窗外泥泞的花坛里像是埋着什么黑色的东西。

准备婚礼的这一周，各地发生了瘟疫。奇怪的是，比起平民，贵族们受灾更严重，而且似乎地位越高越容易被瘟疫看上。这里的国王，那里的王储，某大都会的侯爵，哪座城堡的王子，包括上回参加晚会的公爵，一个个都遭了殃。政治局势每天都发生翻天覆地的变化。婚礼前夜，王都的信使连夜赶到伯爵宅邸，信件是由仅担任了三天的摄政王写的，主要内容如下：

……贵族们一致推举您加冕为王，并极力建议将您的乘龙快婿，屠龙者杰克，立为王储……

婚礼举行地十分顺利，所有有幸到场的人都欢呼雀跃。杰克和艾米莉光彩照人，非常恩爱，许多人从四面八方赶过来一睹屠龙者的英姿，平民们预感到小镇会因为这桩婚事而繁荣起来，当地贵族们兴奋地计算着自己的头衔、土地和税收的增长，幸存的大贵族也风尘仆仆地赶过来拍马屁，伯爵或者说准国王本人更是喜出望外。婚礼上，伯爵宣布：“……过些日子我将前往王都加冕，这座城镇就封给我的女儿和女婿……我还请了王国里最棒的雕塑家为我们共同的英雄杰克，塑造描绘他屠龙事迹的雕像——换掉喷泉中心那只病怏怏的猫，即日开工！”所有人都为杰克献上了祝福，但杰克本人却忧心忡忡。

当天夜里，杰克等艾米莉睡熟后，拿着那柄“屠龙”的阔剑，独自前往镇中心的广场。因为戒指的力量，杰克手中的武器都能削铁如泥。杰克走到喷泉前，提起阔剑，隔空向着猫塑像用力一挥，塑像即刻被拦腰斩成两段。平整的切口中间，露出了一顶金冠顶部的轮廓，石缝里则隐约透出了几缕红光。杰克刚想上前取出王冠，大地忽然颤动起来，一股墨色的浊流从水池里升腾起来，绕着残余的半截塑像旋转交汇。浊流中心有张羊皮纸渐渐扭曲收缩成一团，燃烧了起来。羊皮纸烧尽的时候，浊流竟汇成了一个人形，那人一身深棕色的探险家装束，只有一双白色手套格外晃眼。他起初低着头，双脚绷紧，只是手指生硬地活动了几下。浊流散去后，那人在空中颤动了一下，突然抬起了头，脸上挂着诡异的夸张的笑，眼窝里没有眼珠。

“想我了吗，杰克。我是你的兔子，沃格啊。”人形的沃格张开了双臂，语气嘲讽地说道，“是时候物归原主了。”沃格双拳一捏，杰克的戒指就化成一束红光飞进了沃格的眼窝里，另一束红光也从伯爵宅邸的方向飞了过来。杰克浑身无力，瘫坐在地上，惊得说不出话来。取回双眼的沃格，飞向了高空，俯视小镇，而后又飞到了杰克身旁。

“噢噢噢，我亲爱的主人！不要惊慌！我给你讲个故事吧，”一段熟悉的开场白后，沃格绘声绘色地讲了起来，“有位有名中年炼金术士到南方的国度游学，那里的国王盛情款待了他。宴会上，国王的红宝石戒指和炼金术士的红宝石项链发出了奇妙的光芒，到场的来宾都十分惊异。那夜，国王派刺客刺杀炼金术士，自己却疏于防范，被炼金术士抢走了戒指。第二天，王都就被突如其来的沙尘暴掩埋，王国也在一夕之间土崩瓦解。炼金术士意识到，项链和戒指并不会赋予人力量，而是贪婪地把佩带者所拥有的生命或权力据为己有，然后与他们共生。所以炼金术士借用的是项链里巨龙的生命，而国王可能借用了哪位或哪几位古代王者的权力。炼金术士知道，一旦失去它们，自己将一无所有，于是他把戒指和项链封印在了自己的双眼里。就这样炼金术士度过了漫长而又寂寞的时光，找寻生命和宇宙的意义。正当他疲惫地想要进入长眠时，路经一个小镇，镇上最大的商户热情招待了他。那家的主人看似不务正业，却对炼金术颇有见地，甚至可以称得上天才，他邀请炼金术士一起研究家族祖传的一顶金冠。炼金术士这才发现，原来还有一颗可以吸收财富的红宝石，而且这颗宝石的所有权很奇怪，它并非属于商人，而是属于这个家族。所以炼金术士并不能夺走金冠，因为它有一部分属于地底的亡灵。本来几乎看破尘世的炼金术士，突然感到了恐惧，他开始害怕自己的红宝石被商人夺走，因此甚至不敢直视商人的眼睛。炼金术士夜不能寐，他不敢轻举妄动，于是制订了缜密的计划。他向商人提议：‘我们一起研究死神的契约吧，为此我们得先定立一个……我也知道你们商人总会被贵族压榨，为了感谢你协助我研究，我帮你同镇上的伯爵攀个亲家吧，你的儿子和伯爵的女儿虽然还小，但年龄刚好合适，我好歹算个有名望的炼金术士，伯爵不会不给面子……所以契约的内容就是：我帮助你的儿子同伯爵的女儿成婚，为此你要把金冠的所有权交给你的儿子。当然，后面那句其实是废话，只是为了让契约成立。’”商人一开始并没有看出其中的阴谋，而且看起来对这项研究非常感兴趣，于是爽快地签订了契约。就在炼金术士带着商人的儿子去伯爵家提亲的时候，商人偷偷翻看了炼金术士的手记，得知了项链和戒指的存在。察觉到异样的商人，为了阻止邪恶的炼金术士伤害自己的孩子，连夜请人雕刻了象征死神的猫塑像，作为封印的盖板——真是难得的天才啊——他还借用了广场和喷泉的形状构筑了炼成阵，以红宝石金冠为媒介沟通了另外两颗红宝石封印了炼金术士的大部分力量，这样还不够——简直堪称神来一笔——商人从死神那里取得了祖辈们拥有的王冠所有权，以此主动撕毁了契约，让炼金术士也连带无法完成契约。因为毁约，死神夺取了商人家族里所有人的生命，当然除了契约本来的获益者，也就是商人的儿子。炼金术士也差一点被死神剥夺生命，只能献祭了自己身为人的躯壳，勉强依靠两颗宝石苟活，默默地等待复仇。终于有一天，商人的儿子和伯爵的女儿在这命运之地相会了。后面的故事，你是知道的……”

艾米莉躺在床上，奄奄一息，衰老像疯长的藤蔓缠住了她。死亡从指尖开始蔓延，艾米莉觉得手臂麻木，渐渐失去了知觉。她睁开眼，发现杰克不在身旁，只有几只红色的气球静静地躺在地板上，一动不动——“嘭！”，艾米莉从梦中惊醒，气球的碎片散落在桌上。艾米莉赶忙抽出了枕在头下的双臂，揉了揉惺忪的睡眼，打了个哈欠，自言自语道：“原来是看书的时候睡着了啊。这样吧，去杰克那里再买个气球吧。”

艾米莉起身合上书，出门了。书名竟然斑驳得难以辨认，大概是一本关于炼金术的书吧。
entry.summary: 很久很久以前，有一座宁静平和的小镇依山而建。镇中心有个圆形广场，广场中心是一座喷泉，喷泉中心则雕刻着一只神态端庄的短毛猫——这只猫注视着的广场和由广场通向镇上的大小街道，是受商旅们眷顾的地方。每到礼拜日，就会有成群结队的商人远道而来，让这个略显偏僻的小镇热闹非凡。

entry.title: Hexo NexT主题配置补遗
entry.content:
说明

前些天刚学着用Github+Hexo搭建了自己的博客，选了广受欢迎的NexT主题。配置主题的时候遇到了许多问题。好在网上有各个dalao们的文章铺平道路，否则必然会折腾得死去活来。
dalao们也许是万能的，但总归有涉及不到的地方，本文就一些网上没有提到的配置问题稍作补遗。

修改底部logo栏中的爱心图标

关于修改底部的logo栏的详细内容可转到

Hexo-Next底部powered by的logo栏更改以及注意事项

而要修改年份和作者中的爱心图标：

通用方法

打开 hemes ext\layout\_partials ooter.swig
找到

把中间的<i class="fa fa-heart"></i>改成你想要的fontawesome图标
如修改成

就可以成为一名伟大的太阳骑士！

新版方法

打开 hemes ext\_config.yml
找到

把heart改成你想要的fontawesome图标名如：sun-o

附fontawesome图标库

背景动效和优化手机端显示

关于添加背景动效的详细内容可转到

搭建博客使用hexo的next主题如何添加动态背景

问题

添加背景动效以后，从手机端看起来会显得十分杂乱无章，线条也有些模糊。推荐改成白色背景使页面变得简洁。

方法

使用的是Pisces主题

打开 hemes ext\source\css\_schemes\Pisces\_layout.styl
找到.content-wrap内

大括号内添加一行background : white;

使用的是Muse主题

打开 hemes ext\source\css\_schemes\Muse\_layout.styl
找到.header-inner, .container .main-inner, .footer-inner内

大括号内添加一行background : white;

增加百度HTTPS搜索引擎验证

关于增加搜索引擎验证的详细内容可转到

HEXO next 主题增加搜索引擎验证

百度现在支持https网址的验证，打开HTTPS认证
按网页提示步骤操作，获得验证信息。
然后，打开themes/next/layout/_partials/head.swig
找到

用验证的信息补全。
并打开themes/next/_config.yml，添加一行baidu_site_verification: true
在度娘的页面上进行验证即可。

未完待续
entry.summary:
说明

前些天刚学着用Github+Hexo搭建了自己的博客，选了广受欢迎的NexT主题。配置主题的时候遇到了许多问题。好在网上有各个dalao们的文章铺平道路，否则必然会折腾得死去活来。
dalao们也许是万能的，但总归有涉及不到的地方，本文就一些网上没有提到的配置问题稍作补遗。

entry.title: 一篇影评
entry.content:
鹅窝版本 影评（这个标题是不是很炫 ￣▽￣”）

（写昨天看的#刺客聂隐娘#，名为“影评”的影评，正文挺严肃，就不要太在意标题了……）
出门晚了些，一路跑到电影院，还是迟了5分钟。匆忙中入场坐定，一抬头才发现环亚的logo正缓缓飘过，窃喜。

事先做过点功课，所以并没有对奇特的胶片比例大为惊叹，反倒是开场的黑白画面给我这样的感觉——“活这么大有在电影院看过黑白电影？”

些许铺垫，写隐娘“剑术已成”却仍怀有恻隐之心，在师傅眼里并非一个合格的刺客。之后画面渐暖，转为彩色——春江水暖、鸥鹭斜飞、夕阳西下，镜头定格了，朱红色的标题随即映出，也径自定在那里，而景色瞬息万变，涟漪慢慢散开，枝叶轻轻摇曳，浮云渐渐隐去……

正片开始。对白极少，明明是争锋相对，眼看就要唇枪舌剑一番的场面，两方却只是四目相对，或一切尽在不言中，心领神会后转身离开，或一点寒芒先到，用剑说话；特写极少，大都是一个长镜头，推近再拉远；场景极美，以致角色一入镜都觉得略显多余；节奏极慢，比好不容易平静下来的心跳都慢。动作追求写实，几招内定胜负，所以难免略显笨拙；叙事琢磨地极其简练，表情达意含蓄温婉，得细细思量后才能品出隐含内容。像极了山水画，总是用寥寥几笔勾勒出隽永的意境。

（——令镜头留恋的那些场景仿佛也会一直持续下去。）

“一女子抚琴，心神不宁，琴声续断，略凝神，再起，曰：‘罽宾国国王得一青鸾，三年不鸣，有人谓，鸾见同类则鸣，何不悬镜照之，青鸾见影悲鸣，对镜终宵舞镜而死。’”

有人说这是电影里最重要的隐喻，有人凭这句断言这电影讲的是“孤独”——孤独的公主，孤独的隐娘，孤独的候导——我不敢妄加评价，但的确看到那儿，心里一颤。

青鸾舞镜，顾影自怜，郁郁而终。好一个“孤独”！ ——有为天下苍生的大义而孤独，有因生不逢时造化弄人而孤独，有曲高和寡不被凡人理解的孤独，也有“痴痴地望着填不满的荧幕、听着台湾腔文言、数着零星几句台词、忍着前后座的牢骚和呼噜声，满肚子苦水却又无人言说”的孤独。

然而，这些孤独也是有回报的。不论带来了和平安宁，还是导出了惊世骇俗的电影，都相当值当，于我，收获这样一次观影体验也不亏！叔本华说：“要么孤独，要么庸俗！”公主和候导的高境界的确是有力的佐证，而像我这种小角色则期待着偶尔“庸俗”一下……

影片末，隐娘随磨镜小伙儿在一片欢快的唢呐声中走向了远方。
归途中，我也一路哼着唢呐的调子，要知道，我的旅程还未开始。
